publishedAbstract: - When installing ODF with an IBM Flashsystem storage, we are creating an ODF cluster in Internal Mode where the PVs used by OSD pods are based on a new Storage Class (that we will create during the creation of the StorageSystem) that will create/allocate Volumes on the IBM FlashSystem Storage, so the disks used by OSD pods will be LUNs from the IBM FlashSystem Storage. Latest Instructions for ODF 4.15 are here: [Chapter 3. Deploy OpenShift Data Foundation using IBM FlashSystem](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_openshift_data_foundation/4.15/html/deploying_openshift_data_foundation_in_external_mode/deploy-openshift-data-foundation-using-ibm-flashsystem#doc-wrapper)- At the current moment (June 2024) the installation of IBM operators will fail with ODF 4.14 or 4.15, you may get an error like ~~~ CustomResourceDefintion 'flashsystemclusters.odf.ibm.com' not found. ~~~ OR install plan may fail with the error: ~~~ bundle unpacking failed. Reason: BackoffLimitExceeded, and Message: Job has reached the specified backoff limit ~~~ OR ~~~ $ oc-mirror list operators--catalog=registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.16--package=ibm-storage-odf-operator error: package "ibm-storage-odf-operator" not found ~~~
publishedTitle: ODF 4.15 Install with IBM Flashsystem storage fails, "error: package "ibm-storage-odf-operator" not found"
